name: Embalse de Alarcón — circular desde Valverde de Júcar
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Valverde de Júcar traverse a diverse landscape shaped by the Júcar River and the expansive Embalse de Alarcón. The region features extensive pine forests, agricultural lands with olive and almond groves, and notable limestone formations like the Hoz del Río Gritos. Cyclists can expect a mix of paved surfaces and rural roads, offering varied terrain for exploration. Best touring cycling routes around Valverde De Júcar The most popular touring cycling route is Embalse de Alarcón — circular desde Valverde de Júcar, a 13.4 miles (21.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 34 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers scenic views around the Alarcón reservoir. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Valeria – Roman city ruins – Gritos River Gorge loop from Valverde de Júcar, a difficult 42.4 miles (68.3 km) path. This route combines historical exploration at the Roman city of Valeria with the natural beauty of the limestone Gritos River Gorge. Local touring cyclists also love the Torrubia del Castillo – Cerro Olivilla loop from Honrubia, a 18.3 miles (29.5 km) trail leading through agricultural landscapes and rural roads, often completed in about 1 hour 55 minutes. Touring cycling around Valverde de Júcar is defined by reservoir shorelines, pine forests, and agricultural roads, with options ranging from moderate rides to more challenging routes through gorges. The network offers options for different ability levels, from casual rides to longer, more demanding journeys. The routes in Valverde de Júcar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 25 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Valverde de Júcar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Valverde de Júcar?
A: There are 12 touring cycling routes documented on komoot for the Valverde de Júcar area. These routes offer a variety of experiences, from moderate rides around the reservoir to more challenging journeys through gorges and historical sites.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of touring cycling routes in Valverde de Júcar?
A: The routes around Valverde de Júcar cater to various skill levels. You'll find 9 moderate routes, which are suitable for most touring cyclists, and 3 more difficult routes for those seeking a greater challenge. These often involve more elevation gain or longer distances.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see on a touring bike ride here?
A: Touring cycling in Valverde de Júcar offers a diverse landscape. You can expect to ride along the shores of the expansive Embalse de Alarcón, through extensive pine forests, and past agricultural lands dotted with olive and almond groves. The region is also characterized by limestone formations and riverine woodlands along the Júcar River.

Q: Are there routes that explore the Embalse de Alarcón?
A: Yes, the Embalse de Alarcón is a central feature for many cycling routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1094045670/embalse-de-alarcon-circular-desde-valverde-de-jucar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Alarcón — circular desde Valverde de Júcar&lt;/a&gt;, which offers scenic views around the reservoir. Its sandy beaches, pine forests, and coves provide a picturesque setting for a ride.

Q: Can I combine cycling with exploring historical sites?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in history. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20136101" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valeria – Roman city ruins – Gritos River Gorge loop from Valverde de Júcar&lt;/a&gt; allows you to visit the fascinating &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5404336" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman city of Valeria&lt;/a&gt;, combining outdoor activity with cultural exploration.

Q: Are there any scenic gorges to cycle through?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5404346" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gritos River Gorge&lt;/a&gt; (Hoz del Río Gritos) is a significant natural attraction. You can experience its beauty on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20136101" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valeria – Roman city ruins – Gritos River Gorge loop from Valverde de Júcar&lt;/a&gt;, which integrates this limestone canyon into a more challenging ride.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes?
A: Many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1094045670/embalse-de-alarcon-circular-desde-valverde-de-jucar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Alarcón — circular desde Valverde de Júcar&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24538256" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torrubia del Castillo – Cerro Olivilla loop from Honrubia&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31869759" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molinillo Reservoir – Torrubia del Castillo loop from Honrubia&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Valverde de Júcar?
A: The touring cycling routes in Valverde de Júcar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 25 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the scenic views and diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there other interesting landmarks or points of interest near the cycling routes?
A: Beyond the Roman city of Valeria and the Gritos River Gorge, you can also find other notable landmarks. These include the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6013508" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Garcimuñoz&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7801570" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monument to Jorge Manrique&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6977807" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage and Necropolis&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes pass close to these points, offering opportunities for exploration.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Valverde de Júcar?
A: The region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions during spring and autumn, when temperatures are milder. Summer can be quite hot, especially in July and August, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable. Winter can be cooler, but often still suitable for cycling on sunny days.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring options available?
A: For those looking for a longer journey, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1357793943/return-to-the-alarcon-reservoir-from-honrubia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Return to the Álarcón reservoir from Honrubia&lt;/a&gt; route covers over 93 km, offering a challenging and extensive ride through the region's diverse landscapes.
tips: The town is located on the banks of the Gritos river. On the Santo Niño promenade, you can look out from one of the enabled viewpoints and contemplate the beautiful panoramic view of the river.
The remains of the telegraph tower that was part of the Madrid-Valencia line, is located on a hill, now called Cerro Telégrafo.
A nice, wide beach on the Alarcón Reservoir, 3.5 km from Valverde de Júcar. Ideal place to spend a sunny and hot day. The road to get to the beach is gravel and well signposted.

waypoints.name: View of the Valverde telegraph tower
